title: os-helper skill contract alignment — Removed-marker and handshake awareness
userStory: As the os-helper AI assistant, I want my skill file to document exactly how to handle fact-level profile corrections and how to write the verification handshake, so that I apply Removed markers as deletions and produce the handshake file the wizard polls for.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Signal C in `skills/os-helper/SKILL.md` documents the fact-level `**Removed:**` convention from file-contracts.md §4.3. The action instructions for Signal C must distinguish two bullet types: (a) a bullet prefixed with `**Removed:**` means delete that fact from `context/profile.md`; (b) a bullet without the prefix carries the edited replacement text to apply in place. `grep -n '\*\*Removed:\*\*' skills/os-helper/SKILL.md` returns at least one match inside the Signal C action block.

`skills/os-helper/SKILL.md` gains a subsection documenting the phase-2 verification handshake (file-contracts.md §3.2 / ADR-P6-008): when the user pastes the phase-2 verification prompt, the assistant must (a) answer the user's #1 priority question AND (b) write `docs/data/local/handshake.json` with the exact shape `{ "verifiedAt": "<ISO 8601 timestamp>", "assistant": "<assistant name>" }`, creating the `docs/data/local/` folder if it does not already exist. The exact file path `docs/data/local/handshake.json` appears verbatim in the skill text. `grep -n 'handshake.json' skills/os-helper/SKILL.md` returns at least one match.

The handshake subsection explicitly notes that (a) `docs/data/local/handshake.json` is gitignored and will not appear in `git status`, and (b) the handshake write is preferred but not required — assistants that cannot write files may simply answer the priority question; the user then ticks the manual-fallback checkbox. `grep -n 'gitignore\|manual.*fallback\|cannot write' skills/os-helper/SKILL.md` returns at least one match in the handshake subsection.

technicalNotes: Edit `skills/os-helper/SKILL.md` only — no app code changes in this story. Two edits: (1) Signal C action block (lines ~65–70): after the existing step 1 ('Apply the edited sections from the handoff block to context/profile.md — write the file and confirm when done'), add a clarifying note on the two fact-level bullet conventions from file-contracts.md §4.3: a bullet beginning `- **Removed:** ` (exact string, space after colon) is a delete instruction for that fact; any other bullet in an edited section block carries the replacement text to apply in place. Keep the step numbering and the rest of Signal C unchanged. (2) Handshake subsection: add under the 'Work HQ handoff — wizard-aware early exit' block (after Signal C, before the 'If any of signals A, B, or C are present' line, or as a standalone subsection titled e.g. '### Phase-2 verification handshake'). Document that the phase-2 verification prompt (file-contracts.md §4.4) instructs the assistant to write `docs/data/local/handshake.json` with shape `{ "verifiedAt": "<ISO 8601 timestamp>", "assistant": "<name>" }` after confirming the #1 priority. Note gitignore status and manual-fallback. The AGENTS.md under-200-lines rule does not apply to SKILL.md; however, keep the addition concise (under 15 lines).
outOfScope: Modifying any app code, server functions, or the wizard UI. Changing the handoff payload format or the marker string (governed by file-contracts.md §4.3 — implementer must copy the string verbatim). Adding new handoff signal letters (A, B, C remain; handshake is a subsection within phase-2 handling). Changing AGENTS.md.

verificationPlan.checks: Returns at least one match with a line number inside the Signal C action block, confirming the exact deletion-marker string is documented.
Returns at least one match confirming the handshake file path is documented verbatim in the skill file.
Returns at least one match confirming the gitignore note and manual-fallback caveat are present in the handshake subsection.
